hook.text: Flags, anthems, and borders. Nationalism feels ancient and natural. But most nations are recent inventions.

buildup.text: Nationalism is the political ideology that sees the nation as the primary unit of identity and governance.

discovery.text: Benedict Anderson called nations imagined communities. You'll never meet most members, but you feel connected to them.

twist.text: Nations are constructed through shared myths, media, and schooling. They're made, not found. Identity is political work.

climax.text: Nationalism can unite and liberate, but it can also exclude and oppress. The same force inspires freedom and violence.

punchline.text: Nations are imagined, but their power is real.

quiz.question: What did Benedict Anderson mean by imagined communities?
options: Members feel connected despite never meeting most of each other
